This example includes "optional fades" and "optional distractors" that can be used to make different variants. Try hitting the *New Variant* button to see the problems shift. Complete the code that counts words whose length meets a minimum threshold. Note that there is also a distractor among the available blocks. def stay_focused(name): #pin return f"Focus, {name}!" #pin(1) return f"Good morning, {name}!" #distractor return f"You're so silly, {name}!" #distractor return f"How ya doin', {name}?" #distractor return f"Buenos dias, {name}!" #distractor return f"Good job, {name}!" #distractor return f"Good night, {name}!" #distractor return f"Howdy, {name}!" #distractor def average(nums): #pin avg = __[0]__ #pin(1) __[for]__ n in __[nums]__: #pin(1) __[avg]__ += __[n]__ #pin(2) return __[avg]__ #pin(1)
